mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with rising industrial production indicates robust demand-pull inflation and healthy corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress, a sustained increase in the industrial production index signals capacity restoration and easing input cost pressures, which compresses risk premiums and lifts equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO rises during identified supply disruption periods, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a cooling real estate market that reduces construction-related inflationary pressure, prompting rate cut expectations and boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative or flat when HOUST drops > 5% MoM, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annually reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not positively correlated with quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0%, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.025 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in the federal funds rate indicate a labor market that can absorb tighter monetary policy without triggering a recession, supporting equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPI exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the negative predictive relationship disappears after adjusting for FEDFUNDS hikes.
